title: Use-After-Free, Double Free, and Stale Aliases · Undefined-Behavior Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted generation-checked handle repair, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in generation-checked handle repair and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace allocation, publication, last valid use, deallocation, stale use, and address reuse to diagnose use-after-free.
Prevent double free by assigning exactly one release responsibility per live ownership state.
Invalidate pointers to subobjects and one-past positions when the containing allocation lifetime ends.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use generation counters, handles, quarantine, or delayed reclamation where stale asynchronous references cannot be excluded.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Quarantine Generation Defense, challenge the documented misconception with changed-size, failure-injection, printed invariant, compiler, sanitizer, analyzer, fuzz, ABI, concurrency, or production-transfer evidence: Address uniqueness across time is a dependable object identity.
